title: Cortex XDR Incident
description: Schema for a Cortex XDR incident object returned by the Cortex XDR API. Incidents are aggregated collections of related alerts that represent a potential security threat. They are automatically created by Cortex XDR's analytics engine when correlated alerts meet grouping criteria, or manually created by analysts. The incident object provides a unified view of the attack story, including affected hosts, users, alert severity breakdown, and investigation status.

incident_id.description: Unique identifier for the incident, assigned by Cortex XDR upon creation. This ID is used to reference the incident in all API operations including updates, status changes, and alert associations.

incident_name.description: Human-readable name of the incident, typically auto-generated from the correlated alerts or assigned by an analyst. Provides a concise summary of the detected threat activity.

creation_time.description: Timestamp of when the incident was first created, expressed as Unix epoch time in milliseconds. This corresponds to the time the first correlated alert triggered incident creation.

modification_time.description: Timestamp of the last modification to the incident, expressed as Unix epoch time in milliseconds. Updated whenever incident properties change, new alerts are added, or the status is modified.

status.description: Current investigation status of the incident. 'new' indicates an untriaged incident, 'under_investigation' means an analyst is actively working on it, and the resolved statuses indicate the final determination and closure reason.

severity.description: Overall severity of the incident, determined by the highest severity alert or manually set by an analyst. Severity drives prioritization in the incident queue and can trigger automated response actions.

assigned_user_mail.description: Email address of the analyst or user currently assigned to investigate the incident. Null or empty when the incident is unassigned.

assigned_user_pretty_name.description: Display name of the assigned analyst, as configured in their Cortex XDR user profile. Provides a human-friendly label for the assignee.

alert_count.description: Total number of alerts correlated and grouped into this incident. Includes alerts of all severity levels and from all detection sources.

low_severity_alert_count.description: Number of alerts within the incident that have a low severity rating. Low severity alerts typically indicate suspicious but low-risk activity.

med_severity_alert_count.description: Number of alerts within the incident that have a medium severity rating. Medium severity alerts indicate potentially harmful activity that warrants investigation.

high_severity_alert_count.description: Number of alerts within the incident that have a high severity rating. High severity alerts indicate likely malicious activity requiring prompt response.

description.description: Detailed description of the incident providing context about the detected threat, affected systems, and attack techniques. May be auto-generated or manually authored by an analyst.

hosts.description: List of hostnames of endpoints involved in the incident. Includes both source and target hosts identified across all correlated alerts.

users.description: List of user accounts associated with the incident. Includes users whose credentials or sessions were involved in the detected activity across all correlated alerts.

items.description: Username or user principal name of an involved user account.

incident_sources.description: List of detection sources that contributed alerts to the incident. Sources identify the Cortex XDR module or integrated product that generated each alert.

items.description: Name of the detection source (e.g., 'XDR Agent', 'XDR Analytics', 'XDR BIOC', 'Cortex XDR - IOC', 'XDR Analytics BIOC').

rule_based_score.description: Calculated incident score based on Cortex XDR scoring rules. This numeric score is derived from rule-based analysis of alert attributes, indicators, and behavioral patterns. Null when no scoring rules apply.

manual_severity.description: Severity level manually overridden by an analyst, if applicable. When set, this takes precedence over the automatically calculated severity for display and prioritization.

manual_description.description: Description manually provided by an analyst to supplement or replace the auto-generated incident description. Used to document investigation findings or provide additional context.

xdr_url.description: Direct URL link to the incident detail page in the Cortex XDR management console. Provides one-click access for analysts to view the full incident investigation interface.

starred.description: Whether the incident has been starred (bookmarked) by an analyst for quick reference. Starred incidents can be filtered in the incident queue for prioritized attention.

alerts.description: List of individual alert objects that have been correlated into this incident. Each alert represents a discrete detection event from a Cortex XDR module or integrated data source.

Alert.description: An individual alert within a Cortex XDR incident. Alerts are discrete detection events generated by Cortex XDR analytics, BIOC rules, IOC matches, or endpoint agent detections. Multiple related alerts are correlated into a single incident to provide a unified attack story.

alert_id.description: Unique identifier for the alert, assigned by Cortex XDR. Used to reference the specific alert in API operations and correlate with raw event data.

name.description: Human-readable name of the alert describing the detected threat or suspicious activity. Derived from the detection rule, analytics model, or IOC match that triggered the alert.

category.description: Threat category classification for the alert, aligned with the MITRE ATT&CK framework where applicable. Examples include 'Malware', 'Exploitation', 'Credential Access', 'Lateral Movement', 'Command and Control', and 'Exfiltration'.

action.description: The response action taken by the detecting agent or module when the alert was generated. Indicates whether the threat was blocked, allowed, or partially mitigated.

severity.description: Severity level of the individual alert, indicating the assessed risk and potential impact of the detected activity.

host_name.description: Hostname of the endpoint where the alert was triggered. Identifies the specific machine involved in the detected activity.

host_ip.description: IP address of the endpoint where the alert was triggered. May be an IPv4 or IPv6 address, representing the primary network interface of the affected host.

user_name.description: Username of the account associated with the process or session that triggered the alert. Identifies the user context under which the detected activity occurred.

source.description: Detection source that generated the alert. Identifies which Cortex XDR engine, module, or integrated product produced the detection.

detection_timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the alert was detected, expressed as Unix epoch time in milliseconds. Represents the moment the detection engine identified the suspicious or malicious activity.

mitre_technique_id.description: MITRE ATT&CK technique identifier associated with the detected activity, if applicable. Format is 'T' followed by a four-digit number and optional sub-technique suffix (e.g., 'T1059.001').

mitre_tactic_id.description: MITRE ATT&CK tactic identifier associated with the detected activity, if applicable. Format is 'TA' followed by a four-digit number (e.g., 'TA0002').

description.description: Detailed description of the alert providing context about the detected activity, including relevant process information, file details, or network connection data.

event_timestamp.description: Timestamp of the underlying event that triggered the alert, expressed as Unix epoch time in milliseconds. May differ from detection_timestamp when alerts are generated from historical event analysis.
